description_long: This is a classic cast iron radiator with a series of vertical, rounded columns connected by horizontal pipes at the top and bottom. The surface is painted white but shows signs of wear, with patches of chipped paint and some rust visible, giving it a weathered, utilitarian appearance. On one end, there is a prominent manual valve with a round, metallic handle, used to control the flow of water or steam. The radiator stands on four short feet, elevating it slightly above the ground, and the overall construction is robust and heavy. The fins are evenly spaced, forming a repeating pattern that maximizes surface area for heat dissipation. The design is typical of radiators found in older urban buildings, garages, or industrial spaces.
description: A white, weathered cast iron radiator with vertical columns, chipped paint, and a manual valve on one end. Its robust, utilitarian design features evenly spaced fins and a heavy, solid build.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the radiator's narrow profile, rounded column ends, and the alignment of the vertical fins.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of the radiator, the manual valve on the right, and visible chipped paint and rust patches.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view with a visible valve at the bottom, showing the radiator's depth and the alignment of the columns.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1 but with the valve on the left, highlighting the uniformity of the vertical fins and surface wear.
attribute_pipe_connections: two, one at bottom left and one at bottom right, horizontal orientation
